title: vesalea subcoriacea
intro: Vesalea subcoriacea, also known as the "Australian Bush Guelder", is a plant species native to the eastern coast of Australia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis, asthma, and coughs. The plant contains compounds with anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which may help alleviate symptoms and support immune function. Vesalea subcoriacea can be prepared as a tea or infusion by steeping the leaves in hot water, making it a popular herbal remedy for respiratory health.
